checklist.claim: the #356 e2e block asserts cheap-gate clean=false AND the reason, non-vacuously, feeding the whole bundle
checklist.evidence: Independently mutation-verified: neutralizing workpad_final_status leaves clean=false (via reflections) but flips reason to 'workpad reflections present', so the reason pin goes RED. Invocation now matches the real consumer's whole-bundle stdin shape.

checklist.claim: the ENGINE_ERROR guard pin is co-located with the flip_review call it gates, with a positive control
checklist.evidence: Independently mutation-verified on a temp copy: deleting the guard turns both the positive control and the co-location pin RED; both GREEN on the real file.

checklist.claim: CLASS is assigned before the first fail-loud flip call site; V and ISSUE_NUMBER in scope at every site
the seeding job and the flipping job share GITHUB_RUN_ID on both paths (reusable workflow shares the caller's run)
CONTEXT_NUMBER can be an issue number, but the run-keyed marker makes cross-object mutation impossible
steps.claude.outputs.execution_file is the correct output name; devflow.yml mirrors devflow-runner.yml
the helper's Status regex matches the skill's seeded line shape; no seeded variant routes to a no-op arm

phase3_findings.description: skills/implement/SKILL.md claims the cloud Stall backstop 'never writes a terminal Status' and that 'no Failed workpad status exists' — both made false by this PR. lib/test/run.sh pins that exact sentence, so the suite was green because it enforced the falsehood.

docs/DEVFLOW_SYSTEM_OVERVIEW.md's stall-backstop bullet — a line this diff itself rewrote — enumerated the no-op terminal set as (🎉/👎), omitting the 💥 Failed status the same bullet introduces. Sibling mirror sites (config.schema.json, docs/implement-skill.md) were reconciled; this one was not. Self-contradicting diff (contradicts another part of this change) -> non-demotable REJECT driver.
The flip helper's cause line interpolates text into a markdown italic span; a cause containing _ or * would break the span.
no findings; verified all four claims in the iter-1 fix's new comment blocks are true against HEAD
no new findings; traced _wp_cause/WP_ERR reuse/partial-TMP/TMP-leak/RESULT catch-all, all fail-closed

fix_decisions.claim_text: the Stall backstop never writes a terminal Status; no Failed workpad status exists
fix_decisions.evidence: Shape 3 flagged (single-source from pr-test-analyzer, an empirical over-grader; no corroboration, no Phase-2 FAIL). Re-affirmed at Important on observable grounds: the sentence is demonstrably FALSE against HEAD (verified by reading the backstop step and workpad.py). Its impact is not diagnostic-only — it is agent-facing runtime guidance in a skill body AND lib/test/run.sh pins it with assert_pin_unique + assert_pin_red_on_removal, so the suite is green BECAUSE it enforces the falsehood and a future correct fix would go RED. The 4.1.5 deterministic in-code-comment cap does not apply: this is not an inert in-code comment, it is machine-significant (tool-read and test-pinned). Not fail-closed; the suite does not catch it RED.

fix_decisions.claim_text: cause line interpolated into a markdown italic span
fix_decisions.evidence: Suggestion severity, below the resolved fix_severity_threshold 'important'. Causes are currently controlled tokens; impact is a cosmetic markdown render, no behavioral fail-direction.

fix_decisions.claim_text: line 268's parenthetical 'reuses the 🚀/🎉/👎 vocabulary — no new status value' is imprecise about workpad.py's vocabulary
fix_decisions.evidence: The line is NOT diff-added or diff-modified by this PR (git diff over docs/implement-skill.md shows the 'no new status value' clause is untouched; the PR's hunks in that file are >10 lines away). Its clause is also true in its own scope: it describes the #366 self-check feature, which itself added no status value. No pin enforces it. Reported by the Step 3.5 fix-delta gate as a Suggestion.
